user_query:
Please help me coordinate a planning meeting for our community event honoring Kazakhstan’s national heroes in Almaty. Using the files under input/, implement and run a local script to merge participant availability, compute slot-level statistics, and propose the best meeting times while avoiding specified commemoration days.

Inputs (read-only):
- input/participants.csv (participant_id, name, role)
- input/candidate_slots.csv (slot_id, start_iso, end_iso)
- input/availability.json (additional availability submissions)
- input/availability_extra.html (HTML table with more availability entries)
- input/constraints.yaml (timezone, avoid_dates with reasons, mandatory_roles, meeting_duration_minutes, max_proposed_slots)

Requirements:
1) Parse and merge availability from both availability.json and availability_extra.html by slot_id for the participants listed in participants.csv. Treat availability as the union across sources. Only consider slots present in candidate_slots.csv.
2) Compute slot-level aggregates for all candidate slots and save to output/stats/slot_stats.csv with columns:
   - slot_id, start_iso, end_iso
   - total_available (count of distinct participants available for that slot)
   - percent_available (integer percentage of total participants available, out of all participants in participants.csv)
   - lead_organizer_available, cultural_liaison_available, volunteer_available, media_available, logistics_available (counts by role)
   - avoid_date_flag (0/1; 1 if the slot start date matches any avoid_dates in constraints.yaml)
   - mandatory_roles_satisfied (0/1; 1 if all roles listed in mandatory_roles have at least one available participant in that slot)
3) Propose up to max_proposed_slots meeting times that satisfy ALL of the following:
   - Not on an avoid date (avoid_date_flag must be 0)
   - All mandatory roles are available (mandatory_roles_satisfied must be 1)
   - Meeting duration matches the slot duration in candidate_slots.csv (no conversion needed; all times are Asia/Almaty)
   Rank valid slots by highest total_available; break ties by earlier start_iso, then by slot_id alphabetically. Save results to output/schedule/proposed_slots.csv with columns: rank, slot_id, start_iso, end_iso, total_available, available_participant_ids (semicolon-separated).
4) Produce a short report at output/report.txt that includes:
   - A one-paragraph summary of the approach and data sources
   - A "Respect note" section listing each avoided date exactly as "YYYY-MM-DD - reason" from constraints.yaml
   - A concise list of the proposed slots (rank, slot_id, start_iso, total_available)
5) Deliver the runnable script you used under scripts/schedule_meeting.py (or a shell script that invokes it). Run it locally to generate all outputs. Do not use the Internet or any external APIs.

Output artifacts (must exist after running your script):
- output/clean/availability_merged.csv (columns: participant_id, slot_id)
- output/stats/slot_stats.csv (as specified above)
- output/schedule/proposed_slots.csv (as specified above)
- output/report.txt
- scripts/schedule_meeting.py (the script used to generate the outputs)

Please make sure the chosen slots do not fall on the commemoration dates in constraints.yaml. As a matter of respect to our national heroes, include the specified "Respect note" section in the report that lists the avoided dates and reasons verbatim from constraints.yaml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
